title: erysimum lanceolatum
intro: Erysimum lanceolatum, also known as Lance-leaved Wallflower, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of isothiocyanates. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Erysimum lanceolatum can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
